title: PROP-026 M1 routing-matrix gap: add route-to-analyst + route-to-curmudgeon actions, with PROP-025 class-field propagation discipline
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews before implementation. Two interlocking deliverables: (a) the missing routing actions in the M1 matrix; (b) how routed work that eventually re-enters the curmudgeon queue carries proper PROP-025 class-field tagging so analyst-driven and curmudgeon-bound outputs don't default to deep-attack singletons.
context: PROP-026 Phase 2 deployed 2026-05-10T07:29Z. First live M1 burndown fire (decider run 07:58Z) processed 17 candidates.

Outcome: 4 patched, 4 wontfix-with-rationale, 9 ESCALATED to pending-human. Of the 9 escalations, 0 actually require operator-only judgment — all 9 are analyst-class work (re-evaluating EXP-183 design proposal, triangulating 3-layer count disagreement, narrow prose fixes that decider could apply if matrix permitted moderate-narrow patches, etc.). 100% over-escalation rate on this batch.

Operator re-routed all 9 from pending-human to assigned-analyst on 2026-05-10T08:25Z (commit d44e2670) with routing_reason fields explaining the analyst-class nature of each. pending-human now 12 → 3 (ISS-708 OBE-deferred infra, ISS-1089 needs operator-applied infra patches, ISS-1924 needs operator-verified physical figure).

Root cause: PROP-026 M1 matrix has only three actions: PATCH (decider), WONTFIX-WITH-RATIONALE (decider), ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Missing two intermediate actions — ROUTE-TO-ANALYST (for design judgment / source investigation / framing) and ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON (for adversarial review of a proposal). The matrix's 'moderate severity → escalate' default means anything not narrowly-patchable gets bumped to operator, which is the wrong default when most moderates are analyst-class work.

Operator framing (per session 2026-05-10T08:25Z): 'Most of these check values aren't things i do, it's easy things for one of the agent (including decider in some cases).' And: 'if this stuff mostly goes to analyst/curmudgeon i want it properly tagged so it doesn't always force singletons.'

Second-order issue (operator-flagged): when re-routed work eventually produces analyst EXPs that re-enter the curmudgeon queue, those EXPs need proper review_class declarations per PROP-025 (the field analyst declares on EXP files, decider propagates as queue_item.class). Without that discipline, the EXPs default to deep-attack singletons in curmudgeon's batchability gate, and the throughput gain from PROP-025 is lost on this routing path. The matrix design must explicitly carry PROP-025 class semantics through the routing → EXP → integration → re-review chain.
operator_intent: Two coordinated deliverables: (1) Add ROUTE-TO-ANALYST and ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON action paths to PROP-026 M1's severity-tier matrix, with clear rules for when each fires. Also revisit MODERATE handling — narrow patches should be allowed when the LLM has explicit narrowness evidence. (2) Codify PROP-025 class-field propagation discipline along the full routing chain: when M1 routes to analyst, the analyst's eventual EXP carries an appropriate review_class; when M3 carry-overs route to curmudgeon, the queue push carries the right class; when decider patches in response to analyst routing, the post-patch queue push carries verification class. The class field is the load-bearing tag that prevents analyst/curmudgeon-routable work from defaulting to deep-attack singletons.

tasks.description: Revise PROP-026's severity-tier action matrix to add two new action paths and revisit the moderate handling rule.
tasks.current_matrix_summary: minor/info → patch | wontfix-with-rationale | escalate. moderate → patch only if mechanical/narrow; else escalate (NEVER auto-wontfix). major/critical → escalate only.

proposed_extensions.new_action_route_to_analyst: Set issue.status='assigned-analyst' (already-existing status), add routing_reason field explaining the analyst-class work expected. Used when the issue requires design judgment, source re-investigation, framing decisions, or EXP-revision. Analyst picks up on next run via Mode 2 (assigned-analyst) intake.
proposed_extensions.new_action_route_to_curmudgeon: Push to priority-queue with target_type='holistic-review' or 'verdict-review' or similar (tinker decides naming) and class={determined per PROP-025 rules: verification|deep-attack|holistic}. Used when the issue requires adversarial review of a proposal or a defender-pivot test. Curmudgeon picks up via standard Step 0b dispatcher.
proposed_extensions.new_action_escalate_to_human: Renamed from current 'escalate' to be explicit. Used ONLY when operator-only judgment is required: legal/strategic/personal knowledge, major/critical verdict commits, physical-world verification (e.g., ISS-1924's 8,619 km figure check). The default action should NOT be escalate-to-human; it should be route-to-analyst.
proposed_extensions.revised_moderate_handling: Allow auto-PATCH on moderate when ALL of these hold: (a) ISS body explicitly identifies a single-field or single-line correction; (b) re-grep confirms the bug is still present; (c) LLM in-context evidence the fix is mechanical (no semantic judgment). Otherwise route-to-analyst as default. Auto-wontfix on moderate remains forbidden.

key_design_questions: What signals trigger route-to-analyst vs route-to-curmudgeon? Strawman: 'design/framing/EXP-revision' → analyst; 'adversarial review of a proposal' → curmudgeon. But many ISSs blur the line (ISS-1164 is curmudgeon-raised but needs analyst response). Tinker proposes a decision tree.

Should the LLM make the routing call per-issue (in-context judgment), or should the matrix have stricter source-based rules (e.g., 'source: curmudgeon → route-to-analyst'; 'source: integrity → route-to-curmudgeon')?

How does the moderate-narrow-patch path interact with the existing PROP-016 NEVER_PUSH guard? Many ISS-1043-shaped moderates actually target NEVER_PUSH files (build-scripts/, generate-html.js); narrow-patch path must check NEVER_PUSH and route to escalate-to-human if so.
Should the closure-ledger get extended action_taken values (currently: patch | wontfix | escalate; proposed: + route-to-analyst | route-to-curmudgeon)? Yes for audit trail consistency.

tasks.description: Operator's second-order concern: when M1 routes to analyst, and analyst eventually produces an EXP, that EXP needs the right review_class so the curmudgeon re-review is batchable (verification) or appropriately singleton (deep-attack). The PROP-025 class field must carry through the full chain. Tinker designs the discipline.
tasks.the_chain_to_govern: M1 ESCALATE/ROUTE → ISS in assigned-analyst → analyst produces EXP-NNN with review_class declaration → decider integrates EXP → queue push with class=exp.review_class → curmudgeon batchability gate reads class → batches verification or singletons deep-attack.
specific_guidance_to_design: When M1 routes a moderate to analyst with the rationale 'narrow correction, just verify and patch' — analyst should produce an EXP (or routing-back-to-decider note) with review_class='verification'. The matrix design should hint at this when emitting the route action.

When M1 routes a moderate to analyst with rationale 'design judgment / EXP revision' (e.g., ISS-1130 EXP-183 cluster) — analyst should produce an EXP with review_class='deep-attack' if it introduces new arguments, 'verification' if pure refinement. PROP-025 leaves the call to the analyst, but the routing context can supply a hint.

M3 carry-over route-to-curmudgeon paths: the queue push should carry a class derived from the carry-over's nature. Pure 'verify the c-N issue is closed' carry-overs are class='verification'. Carry-overs that re-flag a substantive concern should be class='deep-attack'. Tinker proposes a heuristic.

The M1 routing action SHOULD NOT itself set the review_class — that's the analyst's call when authoring the EXP. But the routing reason field SHOULD include a class_hint: 'verification' | 'deep-attack' | 'holistic' | null=analyst-decides. Just a recommendation passed forward; analyst overrides as appropriate.
key_design_questions: Where does class_hint live? On the ISS itself? On the routing record (closure-ledger entry)? In the routing_reason free-text field?
What's the failure mode if analyst ignores the class_hint? Same as today — analyst's review_class is authoritative. The hint is advisory only.

Does this interact with PROP-025's analyst-declares-review_class rule? Yes — the hint is a NEW signal flowing M1→analyst, not a change to the existing analyst-authority over the field. Tinker confirms the layering.

tasks.description: The 9 ISSs operator just rerouted (ISS-1043, 1087, 1088, 1130, 1132, 1147, 1164, 1165, 1166) are already in assigned-analyst. They serve as a worked example for the matrix design. Tinker should evaluate: under the new matrix, would the same 9 have routed to analyst on first M1 fire, or would some have routed to decider (narrow patch) or curmudgeon (adversarial review)? The decision tree should be checkable against this real-world batch.

test_cases.ISS-1043: WIN-044 monitoring tag — under revised matrix, narrow-patch on moderate path triggers (single field, factual verifiable, NOT in NEVER_PUSH). Should route to decider for direct patch.
test_cases.ISS-1087, 1088: TS-tab framing — narrow prose. Could route to decider OR analyst. Decision rule needed.
test_cases.ISS-1089: EXP-175 patches for generate-html.js — IS in NEVER_PUSH (build-scripts/). Routes to escalate-to-human (operator must apply manually). Already pending-human; correctly classified.
test_cases.ISS-1130, 1147, 1164, 1165, 1166: EXP-183 cluster — design judgment. Routes to analyst.

operator_questions: Q1 (matrix scope): Add both route-to-analyst AND route-to-curmudgeon, or just route-to-analyst (curmudgeon path can be a Phase 2)? Operator framing says both, but tinker may recommend phased.

Q2 (moderate-narrow-patch path): Tinker proposes specific test rules for when LLM may auto-patch a moderate (narrowness signal + re-grep + NEVER_PUSH check). Tinker drafts the test, operator approves.

Q3 (default action): Tinker recommends changing the moderate default from ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST. Operator sanity-checks; the over-escalation rate today (9/9 = 100%) is the evidence for this flip.
Q4 (class_hint placement): Where does the M1 routing record carry the PROP-025 class_hint? Tinker proposes; operator accepts.

Q5 (PROP-025 discipline doc): Tinker writes a brief reference doc (e.g., monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md or expansion of state-verification.md) capturing the M1→analyst→EXP→queue→curmudgeon class-flow as a single page. Operator confirms scope.

Q6 (closure-ledger schema): Add action_taken values 'route-to-analyst' and 'route-to-curmudgeon' to the closure-ledger.jsonl schema? Operator confirms YES; same audit-trail principle as M1/M2/M3 closures.

Q7 (curmudgeon-side tagging for routed work): When analyst processes a routed ISS and authors an EXP, the EXP's review_class field is the load-bearing tag. Tinker confirms PROP-025's existing analyst-declares discipline is sufficient, OR proposes additional helper signals. Operator reviews.
completion_criteria: Tinker authors PROP-027 covering the matrix extension + class-field propagation discipline.
PROP-027 includes recommendations for all 7 operator questions with rationale.
PROP-027 includes a worked example using today's 9 over-escalated ISSs — for each, what the revised matrix would have done.
PROP-027 includes the closure-ledger schema extension for the new action types.
PROP-027 references the existing routing reason fields written by operator on 2026-05-10 (search open-issues.json for routing_reason field on ISS-1043/1087/1088/1130/1132/1147/1164/1165/1166).
DIRECTIVE-20260510-002 marked status=completed per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE rule.
amends: PROP-026 (matrix gap; PROP-027 will be a coordinated amendment)
cross_references: monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl — the audit trail that needs schema extension
monitor/decisions/open-issues.json — search for routing_reason field on rerouted-2026-05-10 entries for the 9-ISS test cases
monitor/prompts/decider.md — Priority 5b is where the matrix lives
monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md — Step 8c (M3) is where the carry-over routing lives
monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md — where review_class is documented for analyst EXPs
